title: Intensely Charred American Chestnut Mushroom Casserole
description: A cozy American dish featuring tender chestnut mushrooms with an intense char, perfect for a flexitarian dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium high heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they are intensely charred, about 5 minutes per side.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the bhuna spice mix and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked mushroom mixture, salt, and black pepper.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, arrange half of the mushroom mixture in the bottom of the dish.
Sprinkle half of the grated cheddar cheese and half of the whole wheat breadcrumbs over the mushroom mixture.
Repeat the layers, starting with the remaining mushroom mixture, then the remaining cheese, and finally the remaining breadcrumbs.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the casserole.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
